top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: Conflict still leads the day. Israel pushed its Lebanon offensive to its deepest point since 2006, with troops reaching the edge of Nabatieh after advancing beyond the Litani River, even as Israeli and Lebanese officers held talks at the Pentagon. In Singapore, US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth used the Shangri-La Dialogue to warn the US is 'more than capable' of resuming strikes on Iran if nuclear talks fail, while Zelensky warned of a looming 'massive' Russian attack. At home for US readers, a bus driver was charged with involuntary manslaughter after a deadly I-95 crash in Virginia killed five.

stories.headline: Israeli troops reach edge of Nabatieh after advancing beyond Lebanon's Litani River
stories.summary: Israeli forces pushed beyond the Litani River — their deepest advance into south Lebanon since 2006 — and fought Hezbollah in villages on the outskirts of Nabatieh, a major regional city, as Israeli and Lebanese officers held their first security talks since April at the Pentagon. Lebanon's army said a hostile Israeli drone seriously wounded two of its soldiers near Nabatieh and a separate strike killed a paramedic in Jebchit; Hezbollah said it fired rockets at Kiryat Shmona. (Israeli advance; Lebanese-reported casualties.)
stories.source: Al Jazeera / Washington Times (AP) / CBC News

stories.headline: Hegseth says US 'more than capable' of resuming strikes on Iran if talks fail
stories.summary: At the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Washington is prepared to restart military action against Iran if negotiations to extend the fragile ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz collapse, saying US forces and stockpiles remain 'more than capable'. He said Trump was showing 'patience' and would only accept a 'great deal' to keep Iran from a nuclear weapon. An adviser to Iran's Supreme Leader accused Trump of 'betraying diplomacy'.

stories.headline: Zelensky warns Russia is preparing a 'massive' new attack on Ukraine
stories.summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky said Ukraine has intelligence that Russia is preparing a new large-scale strike, urging residents to heed air-raid alerts and renewing calls for more US Patriot systems. The warning follows Russia's May 24 record bombardment of the Kyiv region, which Ukraine said involved about 600 drones and 90 missiles, including a hypersonic Oreshnik. No new mass strike had been reported as of this edition.

stories.headline: Bus driver charged with manslaughter after deadly I-95 crash in Virginia kills five
stories.summary: A tour-bus driver was charged with two counts of involuntary manslaughter, with more charges pending, after the bus failed to slow for a work-zone queue on I-95 in Stafford County, Virginia, striking several vehicles and killing five people — including two children — and injuring 44. Federal investigators said they are examining the driver's speed, possible fatigue and the work-zone design.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: It's the closing day of the Shangri-La Dialogue (May 29–31), which Singapore is hosting — and it's dominating local headlines. US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth used his address here to press Asian allies on China and warn the US could resume strikes on Iran, while Defence Minister Chan Chun Sing is set to give a plenary today on security partnerships 'in a fragmenting world'. Otherwise it's a quiet Sunday: expect a hot, humid day with highs near 35°C and a good chance of afternoon thundery showers.

stories.headline: At Singapore's Shangri-La Dialogue, Hegseth presses allies on China and warns Iran
stories.summary: Speaking at the Singapore-hosted Shangri-La Dialogue, US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said there is 'rightful alarm' over China's military build-up and urged Asia-Pacific partners to lift defence spending, while striking a milder tone on Beijing and again not naming Taiwan. He also said the US was 'more than capable' of resuming strikes on Iran if talks fail.

stories.headline: Shangri-La Dialogue wraps up; Chan Chun Sing to speak on partnerships in a 'fragmenting world'
stories.summary: The 23rd Shangri-La Dialogue concludes today, with Singapore's Defence Minister Chan Chun Sing scheduled to address a plenary on 'Evolving Security Partnerships in a Fragmenting World' and to host Five Power Defence Arrangements ministers to breakfast. Organised by the IISS, this year's forum drew 44 countries and 54 ministerial-level delegates.

stories.headline: Hot and humid Sunday with afternoon thundery showers likely
stories.summary: NEA's Meteorological Service 24-hour forecast (issued 5:31am, May 31) is for partly cloudy, warm conditions with a fairly high chance of thundery showers, mainly in the afternoon. Temperatures are expected to range from about 25°C to a high near 35°C, with light to moderate southerly winds of 10–20 km/h. Lightning risk accompanies the showers.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: All three theatres are tense. In Lebanon, Israel escalated sharply — on May 30 its troops pushed beyond the Litani River to the edge of Nabatieh, the deepest advance since 2006, even as both sides met at the Pentagon. In the Gulf, talks on a 60-day ceasefire framework have stalled: Hegseth warned the US is 'more than capable' of resuming strikes on Iran while Tehran accused Washington of bad faith, and the Strait of Hormuz stays effectively shut, drawing down world oil stocks at a record pace. In Ukraine, no new mass strike yet, but Zelensky warned Russia is preparing a 'massive' attack after its record May 24 bombardment of Kyiv.

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine War
theatres.status: No new mass strike yet; Kyiv warns of an imminent 'massive' Russian attack after the record May 24 bombardment

timeline.event: One of Ukraine's largest drone strikes on Russian territory kills at least four people, including three near Moscow, and wounds about a dozen, local authorities say.

Ukraine says drone strikes killed at least 65 Russian drone-pilot cadets and an instructor at a training camp in occupied territory; Zelensky says a separate strike killed and wounded nearly 100. (Ukrainian claims; Russia had not confirmed tolls.)

Russia hits Kyiv with its largest attack on the capital of the war — Ukraine's Air Force says about 600 drones and 90 missiles were launched, including a hypersonic Oreshnik that struck Bila Tserkva; defences downed or jammed most. Kyiv officials report several killed and dozens injured. (Russia did not dispute the strike.)
A Russian drone crashes into an apartment building in Galati, eastern Romania, wounding two — Romania summons Moscow's ambassador and NATO/EU condemn the incursion, raising spillover concerns.

Zelensky says Ukraine has intelligence that Russia is preparing a new 'massive' strike on its cities, urging residents to heed air-raid alerts and renewing calls for more Patriot systems. No new mass strike had been reported as of this edition. (Latest state of play.)

theatres.name: US/Israel-Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Ceasefire-extension talks stalled; US threatens to resume strikes, Iran pushes back; strait still effectively closed

timeline.event: US and Israel launch a coordinated air war on Iran, striking military, nuclear and leadership sites; Iran retaliates with missiles and drones on Israel, US bases and Gulf states.
Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz 'closed' and threatens to attack ships transiting it; the IRGC boards merchant vessels and lays sea mines, choking the key oil chokepoint.
Trump says a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is 'largely negotiated' and will be announced soon.

US and Iranian negotiators reach a tentative 60-day framework to extend the ceasefire, reopen and de-mine the Strait of Hormuz and begin nuclear talks — but it awaits Trump's final approval and Iran has not formally confirmed; the strait remains effectively closed.

The heads of the IEA, IMF, World Bank and WTO jointly warn that global oil inventories are being drawn down 'at a record pace' due to the loss of supply through Hormuz, with growing risks to fuel security ahead of peak summer demand.

At the Shangri-La Dialogue, Hegseth says the US is 'more than capable' of resuming strikes on Iran if talks fail; an adviser to Iran's Supreme Leader accuses Trump of 'betraying diplomacy', underscoring that the framework is not finalised. The strait remains effectively closed. (Latest state of play.)

theatres.name: Israel-Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Lebanon truce collapsing; Israel advances beyond the Litani to the edge of Nabatieh (May 30) as the sides meet at the Pentagon

timeline.event: An October 2025 ceasefire with Hamas takes effect in Gaza, with Israel permitted to temporarily hold roughly half the territory.
Israel and Lebanon agree a US-brokered ceasefire (effective ~April 17), initially a short truce to halt fighting and open negotiations.

Israeli air attacks across southern Lebanon and near the Syrian border kill at least 20 people, Lebanon's Health Ministry says, despite the ceasefire; Israel says it is targeting Hezbollah. (Lebanese toll; Israel cites militant targets.)

Netanyahu says he ordered the IDF to seize 70% of Gaza — beyond the truce terms — saying Israel already controls about 60%; the UN warns it would deepen the humanitarian crisis. In Lebanon, Israel strikes a southern Beirut suburb and southern towns. (Lebanese-reported tolls.)

Netanyahu says Israeli forces crossed the Litani River and seized commanding terrain in southern Lebanon, vowing to crush Hezbollah; Lebanese authorities report new deadly strikes. (Israeli claim; Lebanese-reported casualties.)

Israeli troops reach the outskirts of Nabatieh — their deepest push into south Lebanon since 2006 — fighting Hezbollah in nearby villages, as Israeli and Lebanese officers hold security talks at the Pentagon. Lebanon's army says an Israeli drone seriously wounded two of its soldiers near Nabatieh and a strike killed a paramedic in Jebchit; Hezbollah says it fired rockets at Kiryat Shmona. (Israeli advance; Lebanese-reported casualties. Latest state of play.)
timeline.source: Al Jazeera / Washington Times (AP) / NBC News

outbreaks.label: Outbreaks
outbreaks.ai_summary: Two outbreaks dominate. The DRC/Uganda Bundibugyo Ebola epidemic — a WHO-declared PHEIC since May 17 — keeps growing: as of May 28, DRC reported 125 confirmed cases (17 deaths) plus 906 suspected, and Uganda 9 confirmed (1 death), though authorities revised suspected counts down after a data clean-up. Separately, the MV Hondius Andes-hantavirus cluster holds at 13 cases (11 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths, with the ship now disembarked in Rotterdam and over 600 contacts traced across 32 countries; no confirmed onward spread off-ship. Public risk outside affected settings remains low.

outbreaks.name: DRC / Uganda — Bundibugyo Ebola (PHEIC)
outbreaks.status: PHEIC since 17 May; confirmed counts still rising
outbreaks.scope_note: Lab-confirmed only, DRC + Uganda combined: DRC 125 confirmed / 17 confirmed deaths, Uganda 9 confirmed / 1 confirmed death (per ECDC, citing DRC Ministry of Health / WHO, data as of 28 May 2026). DRC also reports 906 suspected cases with 223 deaths among suspected; suspected counts fell vs the prior report after a DRC data revision. No licensed vaccine exists for the Bundibugyo strain.

outbreaks.name: MV Hondius — Andes hantavirus
outbreaks.status: Limited person-to-person spread aboard ship; off-ship risk low
outbreaks.scope_note: 13 cases = 11 lab-confirmed Andes virus (ANDV) + 2 probable; 3 deaths (WHO Disease Outbreak News, latest update late May 2026, corroborated by ECDC). All cases are MV Hondius passengers/crew from a single vessel; the '32' is the number of countries/territories tracing 600+ contacts, NOT countries with cases. The ship disembarked in Rotterdam on 18 May; its 23 crew entered quarantine there.

stories.headline: DRC Bundibugyo Ebola tops 125 confirmed cases as PHEIC response continues
stories.summary: As of 28 May, the DRC Ministry of Health reported 125 confirmed Ebola (Bundibugyo) cases with 17 deaths and 906 suspected cases (223 suspected deaths); Uganda reported 9 confirmed cases and 1 death. ECDC notes suspected figures were revised down after a DRC data clean-up. WHO declared the epidemic a public health emergency of international concern on 17 May. There is no licensed vaccine for the Bundibugyo strain.
stories.source: ECDC (citing DRC Ministry of Health / WHO)

stories.headline: WHO chief visits Congo, says rare Ebola outbreak 'can be stopped'
stories.summary: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus travelled to Kinshasa to back the response to the Bundibugyo Ebola epidemic, saying it 'can be stopped' even as insecurity in the affected eastern provinces complicates containment.

stories.headline: MV Hondius hantavirus cluster steady at 13 cases, 3 deaths; ship disembarked in Rotterdam
stories.summary: WHO's latest update puts the MV Hondius Andes-hantavirus cluster at 13 cases (11 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths, all among passengers and crew of the single vessel. The ship reached Rotterdam on 18 May and its 23 crew entered quarantine; more than 600 contacts are being monitored across 32 countries, with no confirmed onward community transmission off the ship.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Edge and infrastructure flaws lead this week. An actively-exploited authentication-bypass in Palo Alto's GlobalProtect VPN (CVE-2026-0257) and a newly disclosed Linux kernel 'CIFSwitch' local-root bug both landed May 30 — the Palo Alto flaw is in CISA's KEV and researchers urge treating it as critical despite a 7.8 score. On the breach front, the ShinyHunters gang leaked 4.9 million Charter Communications customer records, attackers abused ChatGPT share links to deliver malware, and Dutch police dismantled a 17-million-device residential-proxy botnet.

stories.headline: Palo Alto GlobalProtect VPN auth-bypass (CVE-2026-0257) now exploited in attacks
stories.summary: Palo Alto Networks confirmed limited active exploitation of an authentication-bypass in PAN-OS GlobalProtect that lets attackers forge override cookies to log in as the local admin; Rapid7 traced successful exploitation to mid-May and urges treating it as critical despite the 7.8 score. CISA added it to the KEV catalog. Affected configs use GlobalProtect with authentication override cookies enabled.

stories.headline: New 'CIFSwitch' Linux kernel flaw gives local root on multiple distributions
stories.summary: Researchers disclosed CIFSwitch, a local-privilege-escalation bug in the Linux kernel's CIFS subsystem that fails to verify the origin of cifs.spnego key requests, letting an unprivileged user forge one and load a malicious NSS module to gain root. Present since 2007, it affects stock builds of distributions including Linux Mint, CentOS Stream 9, Rocky/AlmaLinux 9 and SLES 15; a kernel patch validates request origins.

stories.headline: ShinyHunters leak 4.9 million Charter Communications customer records
stories.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion gang leaked data on 4.9 million Charter Communications accounts after an April vishing attack compromised an employee's Microsoft Entra account and the firm's Salesforce instance. Exposed data includes names, emails, addresses, phone numbers and support-ticket details; Charter says no sensitive personal information or CPNI was taken.

stories.headline: Attackers abuse ChatGPT share links to host fake outage pages that deliver malware
stories.summary: Threat actors are abusing OpenAI's ChatGPT conversation-sharing feature to host convincing fake 'service outage' pages on a trusted chatgpt.com domain, luring victims into downloading malware. The technique borrows the credibility of a legitimate AI platform to slip past user suspicion and some filters.

stories.headline: Dutch police dismantle 17-million-device 'Asocks' residential-proxy botnet
stories.summary: The Hague police cybercrime unit and the Dutch NCSC took down the Asocks botnet — at least 17 million compromised consumer devices worldwide run via about 200 Netherlands-based servers — which had been sold as a 'residential proxy' service to route malicious traffic and launch attacks. The servers were seized and the botnet taken offline.
